title: Implement _TDummyWriteBatch and dummy runTransaction against the in-memory store
blocks: DocumentReference task â€” it shares the in-memory store contract established here.

status_instructions.on_followup: Append to the DocumentReference task's `side_context` if the batch/transaction stubs expose any shared helper it should reuse.

description: Create a library-private `_TDummyWriteBatch implements WriteBatch` and override `runTransaction` on TDummyFirestoreApi<T>. The write batch queues set/update/delete closures as they are added, applies them in order on `commit()`, and re-emits the collection snapshot once at the end. `runTransaction` wires the caller's handler to a `_TDummyTransaction` that reads and writes `_store` directly; commits inline, rollbacks are no-ops. Replace the `UnimplementedError` bodies from task 09 in `createDocInBatch`, `updateDocInBatch`, `deleteDocInBatch`, `createDocInTransaction`, `updateDocInTransaction`, `deleteDocInTransaction` with real implementations that route through the queued closures / transaction helper.
context_for_junior: Firestore supports two bulk-write modes: WriteBatch (enqueue ops, commit them all at once) and runTransaction (read-then-write with conflict detection). Our dummy does not need real conflict detection â€” single-threaded in-memory writes cannot race. What it needs is for callers who already use `WriteBatch` and `runTransaction` to keep working without rewriting their feature code. The batch is a thin queue of closures; on `commit()` we loop through them against `_store`, then fire one collection emission. The transaction is even simpler: invoke the caller's handler with an object that reads and writes `_store` directly, then return whatever the handler returned.
why_it_matters: Several services in the monorepo wrap multi-entity updates in WriteBatch or runTransaction. Without this task, those services crash the first time they touch a batch in dummy mode.
instructions: Create `_TDummyWriteBatch` as a library-private class in the same file (or an additional part file) declared `implements WriteBatch`. Override every method the monorepo actually calls (`set`, `update`, `delete`, `commit`). For other methods the real WriteBatch exposes, override them to throw `UnimplementedError` with a clear dummy-mode message.

`_TDummyWriteBatch` carries a reference to its owning `TDummyFirestoreApi` and an internal `List<void Function()>` of queued operations. Each set/update/delete call appends a closure that applies the corresponding mutation against `_store`.
`commit()` walks the queued closures in order, applies them all, calls `_emitCollections()` and the appropriate `_emitDoc()` calls once per affected id, and returns `Future<void>`.
Override `writeBatch` on `TDummyFirestoreApi<T>` (the getter/method that the real api exposes) to return a fresh `_TDummyWriteBatch` bound to `this`.

Rewrite `createDocInBatch`, `updateDocInBatch`, `deleteDocInBatch`: accept the caller's `WriteBatch writeBatch` parameter and â€” when it is an instance of `_TDummyWriteBatch` bound to this api â€” enqueue the corresponding mutation closure. When the caller passes a real (non-dummy) WriteBatch, throw a clear error pointing the caller at the correct setup. (In practice the dummy api never produces real WriteBatches, so the wrong-type path should be unreachable.)

Override `runTransaction` on `TDummyFirestoreApi<T>`. Construct a `_TDummyTransaction` that implements the real Transaction interface with `get`, `set`, `update`, `delete` delegating to `_store`. Invoke the caller's handler with it, await the result, and return the handler's return value wrapped in the parent class's expected return type.
Implement `createDocInTransaction`, `updateDocInTransaction`, `deleteDocInTransaction` by routing through the `_TDummyTransaction` instance, mirroring the batch-variant contract.

All batch and transaction paths respect the latency + failure gate per the spec's `latency_and_failure_gate.applies_to` list (note: this list includes createDoc/updateDoc/deleteDoc but not the batch/transaction variants â€” double-check the spec before adding the gate; the spec only requires the gate on non-batched calls).
Unsupported Transaction / WriteBatch methods throw `UnimplementedError('dummy-mode: this WriteBatch/Transaction method is not stubbed; override in feature wiring or add it to the dummy api')`.
Run `make analyze package=turbo_firestore_api` and the new unit tests.
goals: WriteBatch and runTransaction work end-to-end against the in-memory store.
Single snapshot emission on commit â€” no N emissions for N queued ops.
Transaction handlers read and write the store inline.
Unsupported operations fail loudly with clear guidance.
non_goals: No DocumentReference stub improvements â€” next task.
No query filter/sort support â€” separate task.
acceptance_criteria: Unit test: enqueue three creates in a WriteBatch, commit, then assert the store has three entries and open collection streams received exactly one snapshot (not three).
Unit test: a runTransaction handler reads an entity, updates one field, returns â€” the store reflects the update after the handler returns.
Unit test: calling an unimplemented WriteBatch method throws UnimplementedError with a message containing the substring 'dummy-mode'.
Unit test: runTransaction returns the handler's return value to the caller unchanged.
constraints: Library-private visibility for both stub classes.
No workarounds for missing Transaction methods â€” throw and document.
Reuse the write preamble helper from task 09 where appropriate; do not copy-paste.
tools: RepoPrompt MCP for inspecting how services in the monorepo use WriteBatch / runTransaction

testing.rules: No mocks. Set up a synthetic DTO + writeable fixture. Use a `@visibleForTesting` hook to count emitted snapshots on a collection stream so you can assert `commit()` emits exactly once.
scenarios: Given an open collection stream, When three creates are enqueued in a batch and commit is called, Then the stream emits exactly one additional snapshot containing all three new entities.
Given a runTransaction handler that updates an entity's field, When the transaction completes, Then the store reflects the update and any open stream emits the updated snapshot.
Given a caller invokes an unimplemented WriteBatch method, Then an UnimplementedError is thrown with a dummy-mode message.
Given a runTransaction handler that returns a specific value, When awaited, Then the caller receives that exact value.
Given a batch that mixes create, update, delete on different ids, When committed, Then the store ends in the expected merged state and one single snapshot is emitted.
testing.refactor_for_testability: If counting snapshots is tricky, expose `_collectionControllers` snapshot count via `@visibleForTesting`.

quality_bar.maintainability: Stub classes are small, single-purpose, and colocated with the dummy api for clarity.
quality_bar.dry: Queued closures reuse the same mutation primitives already in task 09.
quality_bar.single_responsibility: Batch owns queueing + commit; transaction owns handler dispatch.
quality_bar.performance: Single snapshot emission per commit avoids quadratic fan-out cost under big batches.
quality_bar.abuse_resistance: UnimplementedError on unstubbed methods makes missing coverage loud and fixable.
